summary: EFI Core Upgrade Tests - All features verified working. System prompt, safety injection, and classification functions are correctly applied to all EFI endpoints.

test_1_known_model_classification.details: efi_classification.level='AI_GUIDED', color='blue', SAFETY keyword present, BATTWHEELS EFI marker in response

test_3_technician_ai_assist.details: efi_classification field present in response with level and color fields

test_4_cached_ticket_get.details: Cached result includes efi_classification even when serving from cache

test_5_safety_injection.details: SAFETY keyword present in guidance_text for battery-related tickets

test_6_frontend_build.details: Build completed successfully, build folder ready for deployment

unit_test_classify_known_models.details: All known EV models (Ola, Ather, TVS, Bajaj, Tata, Mahindra, Hero) return AI_GUIDED/blue

unit_test_safety_injection_hv_keywords.details: Safety warning injected for battery, BMS, HV, charger, motor controller keywords

unit_test_safety_not_duplicated.details: Safety warning not duplicated if already present in response

seed_data_creation: None - used existing STKT-001 and STKT-003 tickets in battwheels_dev DB
context_for_next_testing_agent: EFI Core upgrade fully tested. Key points: 1) inject_safety_warning() adds safety block if HV keywords detected and SAFETY not already present, 2) classify_efi_response() returns AI_GUIDED/blue for known models (Ola, Ather, TVS, Bajaj, Tata, Mahindra, Hero) and AI_GENERAL/orange otherwise, 3) Both functions applied in generate_guidance() (ai_guidance_service.py:339-355) and ai-assist endpoint (technician_portal.py:776-778), 4) GET /ai/guidance/ticket/{id} adds classification fallback for cached results (ai_guidance.py:252-264). LLM calls take 5-10 seconds. Test tickets: STKT-001 (Ola S1 Pro, battery) and STKT-003 (LocalBrand EcoRider X1, motor).
